weeklySummary.title: Week 1 Summary (Final)
weeklySummary.content: Swim 4x (coaching 900yd + 1,675yd + FORM 2,300yd + Sat PM ~1,200yd) — Run 1x (Sun 13.79mi Central Park!) — Strength 1x (Sat lunch 26min) — Bike 0x
weeklySummary.changes: Changes: Wed bike skipped. Thu swim w/ FORM. Fri run skipped. Sat bike → strength + swim (R calf cramp, swim cut short). Sun long run completed despite heavy legs.
weeklySummary.focus: Highlights: 13.79mi long run (new longest!) with 25 PRs at Central Park. FORM goggles debut. Need to prioritize bike next week. Watch L ankle.

weeklyRecap.title: Week 1 Recap
highlights: 13.79 mi long run at Central Park — new longest training run! 25 PRs despite heavy legs
FORM smart goggles debut — real-time pace display worked great
Swim coaching breakthrough: streamline cues, 1:20-1:40/100yd range
First strength session (lower body) — learned to space calf raises from swim days (R calf cramp)
Zwift recovery ride — good active recovery, legs flushed
Bike training volume = 0 (only recovery ride) — biggest gap to address
weeklyRecap.lookAhead: Week 2 priorities: Get bike training in (indoor or outdoor). Watch L ankle (ongoing lateral pain, no swelling). Keep swim momentum with FORM goggles.

workout.title: AM: Rest (Knee Injury) + PM: Coaching Session
workout.meta: PM: 900 yd | 1:23 | RPE 3 | Coach Paul Capuzzo #CMC
sets: AM: L knee pain - VMO inferior/medial insertion. Icing + rest.
PM: Technical cues + drills to sharpen form
Lots of 50yd drill reps with long rest
workout.note: Knee improved by afternoon. Major streamline breakthrough in coaching.

workout.title: Morning Swim - 1,675 yd
sets: Focused on streamline cues from coaching
Applied drill work from Monday PM session
workout.note: Solid session applying coaching cues. HR stayed controlled.

workout.title: Indoor Ride - Skipped
workout.note: Will make up bike volume later in the week if possible.

workout.title: AM Swim - FORM Goggles Debut
sets: First swim with FORM smart goggles!
Mix of 50s, 100s, and 200s with rest intervals
HR not recorded (FORM Gen 1 limitation - no built-in sensor)
Garmin recorded separately: Avg HR 139 / Max HR 157
workout.note: FORM goggles debut! Real-time pace display worked great. HR doesn't sync underwater on Gen 1 (Bluetooth limitation). Solid 2,300 yd session.

workout.title: Easy Recovery Run
sets: Garmin Coach has Hill Repeats today - saving legs for Sunday long run instead
Focus on form: cadence, posture, relaxed shoulders
workout.note: Light shakeout run. Keep it easy - Sunday's 11mi long run is the priority this week.

workout.title: Lunch Strength + Evening Swim (cut short)
workout.meta: Strength: 26 min | Avg HR 125 | Max HR 156 | Swim: ~1,200 yd | 20 min | Strava
sets: Lower body focus only — skipped core & upper body
R calf cramped mid-swim — had to stop early
Likely from calf raises earlier in the day
workout.note: Bike skipped. Strength was lower body only (~26 min). Swim cut short due to R calf cramp from calf raises earlier. Lesson: space out calf work and swim, or skip calf raises on swim days.

workout.title: Morning 13 Before the Blizzard + PM Recovery Ride
workout.meta: Run: 13.79 mi | 2:14:21 | 25 PRs! | Bike: 4.44 mi Zwift | 20 min | Strava
sets: Avg HR: 162 | Max HR: 182 | Suffer score: 317
Legs heavy from yesterday's strength — wanted to stop but pushed through
Zwift: Whole Lotta Lava in Watopia
Avg HR: 127 | Max HR: 136 | Suffer score: 8
workout.note: Gutted it out on the run — legs heavy from Saturday strength. HR ran high (avg 162). Despite feeling terrible, crushed 25 PRs on hilly Central Park. New longest training run at 13.79 mi. Late night Zwift recovery ride to flush legs — perfect easy spin (HR 127, suffer 8). L ANKLE: ongoing bony tenderness below ATFL, pain at terminal stance/toe off (lateral), no swelling/bruising. Consistent chronic pattern. Lower body very tight/sore (quads, hamstrings, glutes). Monitor ankle through base building.

weeklySummary.title: Week 2 — Illness + 5K PR
weeklySummary.content: Bike 1x · Run 2x (tempo + 5K race) · Sick 3 days · Rest 1x
weeklySummary.changes: Blizzard Mon, sick Thu-Sat. Swim/bike/strength skipped. Rallied for NYRR 5K Sunday.
weeklySummary.focus: Highlight: 5K PR (23:21) despite 3 sick days. All running PRs smashed.

weeklyRecap.title: Week 2 Recap
highlights: Battled through illness — 3 sick days before race
weeklyRecap.lookAhead: Week 3: Recovery from illness. Rebuild swim/bike volume. Don't rush back.

workout.title: Zwift Recovery Ride + Snow Shoveling
workout.meta: 7.15 km (4.4 mi) | 20:48 | 112W avg | HR 127 avg | Suffer 8
sets: Zwift: Easy Hour on Whole Lotta Lava — 20:48, 20.6 km/h, 70m elev
Snow shoveling: 68 min, HR avg 115, max 150
Unplanned low-intensity cardio from blizzard cleanup
workout.note: Blizzard day. Light Zwift spin + extended snow shoveling. Good active recovery volume.

workout.title: Rest Day (Post-Blizzard)
sets: Unplanned rest — snow aftermath + recovery from long week 1
workout.note: Full rest after 7-session Week 1 + snow shoveling Sunday.

workout.title: Tempo 2 Miles + Sauna
workout.note: Quality tempo session. HR drifted to 181 on 2nd tempo mile — watch cardiac drift on longer efforts. Sauna = good recovery.

workout.title: Sick Day — No Training
sets: Sick — unable to work or train
workout.note: Illness hit hard. Full rest, medication, hydration.

workout.title: Sick Day — No Training
sets: Sick — unable to work or train
workout.note: Continued rest. Medication + hydration.

workout.title: Sick Day — Recovery
sets: Feeling better after rest and medication
workout.note: Turning corner. Resting up for 5K race tomorrow.

workout.title: NYRR Washington Heights 5K
sets: Warm-up: 0.80 mi jog to start (7:55, HR 157)
workout.note: Perfect negative split execution post-illness. All PRs from 400m+ smashed. 67m elevation gain — hilly course. Raced smart despite 3 sick days.

weeklySummary.title: Week 3 — Half Marathon Prep
weeklySummary.changes: Adjusted for post-illness recovery + United NYC Half (Mar 15). Running focus, bike paused.
weeklySummary.focus: Build back running legs. Key session: Thu 6-7mi at half pace.

workout.title: Rest — Post-Race Recovery
sets: Hydration focus — still recovering from illness
workout.note: Day after 5K race + still recovering from illness. Complete rest non-negotiable.

workout.title: Evening Swim — 1,625 yd
sets: Planned easy run 4mi skipped — still sick
Couldn't run but got in the water
workout.note: Couldn't run, swam instead. Shorter than planned but good to stay active while recovering.

workout.title: Easy Run — 4 mi Between Patients
sets: HR still elevated post-illness — easy effort felt harder than usual
workout.note: First run back since 5K race. Squeezed in between patients. HR higher than expected for easy pace — still recovering. Good to get miles in.

workout.title: Easy Run — 4.2 mi (Z2 Attempt)
sets: Planned 6-7mi half pace → cut to 4.2mi easy
Tried to find Z2 — mostly stayed in Z3
3 days protein shake only (Switch On diet phase 1) → energy crash
workout.note: Couldn't hold half pace — glycogen depleted from 3-day Switch On diet phase 1. Entering phase 2 tomorrow with carbs at lunch.

workout.title: Swim — 2,200-2,500 yd Steady
workout.note: Day after key run — swim for active recovery. Volume OK, keep intensity steady.

workout.title: Shakeout Run — 3 mi + Strides
sets: 4x20s strides (smooth, not sprinting) with 60s walk between
Strides = leg turnover, NOT speed work
workout.note: Keep legs moving. Strides open up stride length and remind neuromuscular system. Nothing hard.

workout.title: Rest Day
workout.meta: Full rest — taper week starts tomorrow
workout.note: Rest before taper week. United NYC Half in 7 days.

weeklySummary.title: Week 4 — Taper → United NYC Half
weeklySummary.changes: Full taper for United NYC Half Marathon (Mar 15). Minimal volume, fresh legs priority.
weeklySummary.focus: 🏁 RACE WEEK. Stay fresh, stay healthy, trust the training.

workout.title: Rest Day
sets: Light stretching or foam roll if needed
workout.note: Taper week begins. Do nothing hard. Trust the fitness you have.

workout.title: Easy Run — 3 mi Shake-Out
workout.note: Taper run. Short and easy. Resist the urge to go fast.

workout.title: Swim — 1,500-2,000 yd Easy
workout.note: Last swim before race. Easy effort. Flush out legs, stay loose.

workout.title: Shakeout Run — 2 mi + Strides
workout.meta: 2 mi easy + 4x20s strides | Last run before race
sets: This is the LAST run before race day
workout.note: Final shakeout. Strides for neuromuscular activation. Nothing more. Race in 3 days.

workout.title: Rest — Pre-Race
sets: Carb loading: increase carb intake (rice, pasta, bread)
Lay out race gear, check weather forecast
workout.note: Two days out. Rest, eat well, prep gear. No last-minute fitness to gain.

workout.title: Pre-Race Day
sets: 15 min easy walk if antsy — nothing more
Lay out everything: bib, shoes, gels, layers
Set alarm, plan logistics, sleep early
workout.note: Nothing new on race day. Eat what you know. Wear what you know. Sleep early.

workout.title: 🏁 United NYC Half Marathon
sets: Mile 1-4: 8:40-8:45 — HOLD BACK, don't get swept up
HR cap miles 1-8: < 175. After mile 8: race it.
Fuel: gel at mile 5 and mile 9, water at every station
workout.note: Negative split strategy. Post-illness — respect the body but race hard. PR is 1:46, aim sub-1:53 this time. TRUST YOUR TRAINING.
